rationale: The subject property displays a hip-style roof whose shadow length (roughly 30–40% of the visible plane width) and ridge-to-eave ratio — ridge noticeably narrower than the eave footprint but not dramatically so — are consistent with a 6:12 pitch. The roof planes are clearly defined from overhead but still occupy meaningful surface area, ruling out both very shallow (3:12–4:12) and very steep (9:12+) profiles. A lower-slope or under-construction section in the southern portion of the bounding box was excluded as it does not represent the dominant pitch. The Humble, TX (greater Houston) location supports a 6:12 estimate, as warm-climate suburban neighborhoods in this region overwhelmingly favor 6:12 hip roofs, and adjacent comparable homes display nearly identical shadow depth and ridge geometry.
